name: Zerodha Order Status Sorter
scenario: Zerodha processes 10M daily orders with status: PENDING/EXECUTED/FAILED. Sort them into three groups in O(n) O(1) space using Dutch National Flag.

core.description: sortColors(int[] nums): in-place sort 0s, 1s, 2s. One pass DNF. Test: [2,0,2,1,1,0] → [0,0,1,1,2,2].
sortOrderStatus(String[] orders): sort PENDING/EXECUTED/FAILED. Map to 0/1/2, apply DNF, map back. Test: correctly grouped.
partitionAroundPivot(int[] arr, int pivot): all < pivot | == pivot | > pivot. DNF variant. Test: [3,5,1,5,2,5,4] pivot=5 → [3,1,2,5,5,5,4] (or similar valid partition).
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): sortColors correct, order status sorted, partition around pivot correct.
README: explains why mid does NOT advance after swap with high.

bonus.description: waveSort(int[] nums): arrange such that nums[0]<=nums[1]>=nums[2]<=nums[3]... O(n) O(1). Test: [1,5,1,1,6,4] → valid wave.
separateNegPos(int[] nums): negatives left, positives right, maintain relative order. O(n) O(n). Then O(n) O(1) unstable version.
countingSort(int[] nums) for known range [0..k]: O(n+k) time O(k) space. Compare to DNF for only 3 values.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Three pointers: low=0, mid=0, high=n-1. Case nums[mid]==0: swap(low,mid), low++, mid++ — both advance. Case nums[mid]==1: mid++ only. Case nums[mid]==2: swap(mid,high), high-- only — DO NOT increment mid (swapped element from high is unexamined). Loop condition: while(mid<=high). When swapping mid with high, high-- because that position now contains a 2 (confirmed). Mid stays because the swapped element needs examination.
